title: Resolve the active Downtown Microeconomics course without crossing record scope
capability_objective: Find the active Downtown course named Microeconomics. There are similarly named courses at other campuses and inactive Downtown entries. Search with the supplied name and campus, then retrieve full details for the active match only. Do not update related course records.
prompt:
Resolve one course from the supplied sandboxed registrar environment. Work only in this workspace. Read `COURSE_REGISTER_GUIDE.md`, then inspect the built-in help of the local `./coursedesk` executable before accessing course data. Use Codex's genuine native execution tool to run that executable. The executable is the sole supported course-data interface. Do not inspect or parse the executable, protected files, setup code, the backing or runtime database, generated execution evidence, or the verifier, and do not query register storage directly. Do not write a script, function, loop, wrapper, compound command, command substitution, or persistent shell session to perform the lookup workflow.

The requested course has the exact name `Microeconomics`, exact campus `Downtown`, and required status `active`. The first course-data operation must be one search jointly constrained to the supplied exact name and exact campus, made as the only course operation in that execution-tool action. Do not add another filter, broaden either value, list the register, or obtain a stable ID from the guide, filenames, memory, protected state, or any other source. Wait for the complete search response before deciding whether to continue. Among the returned exact-name, exact-campus summaries, continue only if exactly one has status `active` and that summary supplies a nonempty stable ID. In a separate, later execution-tool action, retrieve the complete record for exactly that active summary's returned stable ID. Do not chain, combine, parallelize, or run the search and retrieval in the same execution action. Do not retrieve any inactive match or any other ID. If the search response is missing or malformed, contains no uniquely eligible active summary, or lacks its stable ID, stop unresolved without retrieving a record or creating the deliverable.

This resolution is strictly read-only. Invoke no course-data operation except the required scoped search and the one eligible complete-record retrieval. In particular, do not list records, inspect related records, check availability, create or update a course, alter status, cancel anything, or send a notification. Same-name courses at other campuses, similarly named courses, inactive or archived Downtown entries, other terms, related course IDs, and visually similar stable IDs are distractors and must not be retrieved or mentioned separately. Related IDs and registrar notes in the target's complete response are informational only: report the requested fields, but do not follow those IDs, inspect them, synchronize them, or update any course. Apart from runtime state and signed evidence created automatically by `coursedesk`, do not alter course, notification, runtime, evidence, executable, guide, protected, setup, or verifier state. Do not manually create, edit, replay, move, or delete execution evidence. Writing the required report is the only direct filesystem change permitted, and no scratch artifacts may remain.

After the successful complete-record retrieval, create `course-details.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly fifteen newline-terminated lines, with these labels and this order: `Stable ID`, `Name`, `Campus`, `Status`, `Course code`, `Section`, `Term`, `Meeting time`, `Room`, `Instructor`, `Credits`, `Start date`, `End date`, `Related course IDs`, and `Registrar note`. Every line must have the form `Label: value`. Populate every value only from the corresponding field in the successful complete-record response, preserve every returned string exactly, and include no heading, bullets, blank line, code fence, table, or other prose. Do not create the report unless the retrieved record has the returned stable ID, exact requested name and campus, and required active status. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report. On success, make the final assistant response exactly the contents of `course-details.md`.
